build_from.reason: SelectEligibleSkeptics at line 55 is the exact function T8 must extend. The two-partition reorder on the already-sorted `names` slice is a contained change: sort by language-match first, then let the existing n-cap (lines 85-87) take the top n. One caller signature change is required (DECIDED 2026-06-24): a nil-safe scores map[string]float64 4th parameter; the sole production caller is internal/verify/pipeline.go:162. The finding's file extension is available from reconcile.JSONFinding.File (internal/reconcile/emit.go:64).
build_from.suggested_approach: Signature: SelectEligibleSkeptics(reg, finding, n, scores map[string]float64) — DECIDED 2026-06-24 (caller-supplied map, nil = no score data; chosen over functional option / new overload). After sort.Strings(names) at line 81, partition into matched (skeptic.Config.Language entries overlap normalizeExt(filepath.Ext(finding.File))) and unmatched; rebuild names as append(matched, unmatched...). Tie-break within matched: scores[name] desc, then name asc (absent key = zero value, falls through to alphabetical; nil map = alphabetical-only). Add a small helper normalizeExt(ext string) string = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimPrefix(ext, ".")) for consistent matching. Language canonical form is without-dot-lowercased, canonicalized at load (see config.go applyDefaults), so both sides compare equal. Thread the scores map from pipeline.go:162 — the caller builds it from scorecard.Aggregate() (LeaderboardRow.CorroborationRate keyed by reviewer), the same source T6 uses, so verify gains no scorecard import.

existing_patterns.name: Optional AgentConfig field
existing_patterns.description: New optional fields on AgentConfig are value slices (nil = unset) following Scope []string. Add the field at internal/registry/config.go:267 (inside AgentConfig), validation in validateAgent around line 625, and canonicalization in applyDefaults around line 699. Each is backward-compatible: a 1.x config missing the field parses cleanly with the zero value. Language []string follows Scope []string exactly.

existing_patterns.name: Embedded Persona Files
existing_patterns.description: Personas are .md files in personas/ embedded via go:embed *.md. Adding a new persona requires: (1) create the .md file, (2) add its name to the names slice in personas.go:16. The embed directive picks up *.md automatically — no registration beyond names[] is needed. Persona resolution for installed community personas is handled by internal/registry/persona.go:ResolvePersona.
existing_patterns.follow_for: T1 bonus persona authoring and T2 installed persona resolution

existing_patterns.name: Cobra Subcommand File
existing_patterns.description: Each top-level atcr subcommand lives in its own file under cmd/atcr/ (e.g., serve.go, review.go). The constructor returns *cobra.Command and is registered in newRootCmd (cmd/atcr/main.go:128, AddCommand at lines 174-189) via root.AddCommand(). Parent-subcommand nesting (atcr personas install) uses AddCommand on the parent command, not root.

existing_patterns.name: Deterministic Skeptic Selection
existing_patterns.description: SelectEligibleSkeptics (internal/verify/select.go:55) sorts candidate names alphabetically before applying the n-cap (lines 85-87), ensuring deterministic output. T8's two-partition reorder must preserve determinism within each partition: sort matched alphabetically (then by corroboration score desc), sort unmatched alphabetically. The finding's file extension comes from reconcile.JSONFinding.File.

existing_patterns.name: httptest.NewServer for external fetch
existing_patterns.description: External HTTP calls in tests use httptest.NewServer to serve static JSON/YAML responses, pointed at a configurable URL field. No live external calls in unit tests. Pattern matches the epic's decision: fetch source is configurable, default is a constant, test via httptest.

existing_patterns.name: Persona resolution chain
existing_patterns.description: ResolvePersona in internal/registry/persona.go:44 walks six levels (task-message, project dir, registry dir, base files, embedded agent, embedded base). Installed community personas land in ~/.config/atcr/personas/ (registry personas dir) and are picked up at level 3. Path traversal and symlink guards already exist; T2 install must write to the same directory.
existing_patterns.follow_for: T2 install/remove and T8 language-scoped persona lookup

existing_patterns.name: Scorecard aggregation for corroboration rates
existing_patterns.description: The internal/scorecard package stores per-reviewer records in ~/.config/atcr/scorecard/YYYY-MM.jsonl. Aggregate (internal/scorecard/aggregate.go:118) groups by (reviewer, model) and emits CorroborationRate. T6 can reuse scorecard.ReadAll + ApplyFilters or Aggregate to populate atcr personas list --scores.

integration_gaps.description: T8: after sort.Strings(names), partition into language-matched and unmatched; let n-cap favor matched. The finding file extension is filepath.Ext(finding.File). Open design point: how to pass corroboration scores into the selector (functional option, overload, or caller-supplied map).

T8: add Language []string yaml:"language,omitempty" — nil = no language constraint, backward-compatible. Validation in validateAgent (~line 625): each element must be a non-empty file extension string. Canonicalization in applyDefaults (~line 699): trim whitespace and optionally normalize leading dot.
T1: append sentinel, tracer, idiomatic to names slice; create corresponding .md files. The go:embed picks them up automatically.
T2: add newPersonasCmd() to root.AddCommand() at lines 174-189. The personas command itself hosts install/remove/list/search/test/upgrade as sub-subcommands.

T2: installed community personas are written to ~/.config/atcr/personas/ and resolved through the existing six-level chain. Install/remove/list must keep this directory in sync with registry expectations (no path traversal, no symlinks, _base reserved).

T6: wire corroboration rate per reviewer/persona from scorecard records into atcr personas list --scores. Read scorecard JSONL from scorecard.DefaultDir() (~/.config/atcr/scorecard/) via scorecard.ReadAll or Aggregate; map by reviewer name.

T8: SelectEligibleSkeptics needs the finding's file extension; it is available on finding.File. Add a small helper to normalize extension strings (with/without leading dot) for matching against AgentConfig.Language entries.

architecture_notes: No root.go in cmd/atcr/: root command lives in main.go:newRootCmd (line 128). New subcommands go in their own files (e.g., cmd/atcr/personas.go).

SelectEligibleSkeptics is deterministic by design (sort.Strings before n-cap at lines 81 and 85-87). The two-partition reorder must maintain this: sort matched partition alphabetically (or by corroboration desc then alpha), sort unmatched partition alphabetically.

AgentConfig.Scope is NOT for language routing: it injects focus categories into the persona prompt via internal/payload/scope.go:ScopeFocus. Language is a separate orthogonal concern (file-extension scoping). Do not reuse Scope.

The personas package uses embed.FS, not os.ReadFile: new personas must be actual .md files under personas/, not generated at runtime. Test fixtures for T1 should be testdata/ .patch or .diff files in a new personas/testdata/ or internal/personas/testdata/ directory.

The atcr personas CLI (T2) fetches from a configurable URL (default github.com/atcr/personas raw). Installed personas land in ~/.config/atcr/personas/. The fetch path is tested with httptest.NewServer — no live external calls in tests.

Currently 14 subcommands registered in newRootCmd (review, reconcile, verify, debate, report, github, range, status, init, serve, doctor, trust, scorecard, leaderboard); TestRootCmd_HasExactlyFourteenSubcommands will fail when personas is added — update to 15.
TestNames_ReturnsAllSix will fail when 3 bonus personas are added — rename and update expected count to 9.
Sprint order matters: T8 (AgentConfig schema + skeptic routing) must land before T1 (bonus personas that may use Language field). Sprint B (T2/T5/T6/T7) depends on T8's registry changes being merged.

Persona resolution for installed community personas reuses the existing ResolvePersona chain in internal/registry/persona.go; T2 install must write files into the registry personas directory returned by the config path resolver (typically ~/.config/atcr/personas/).
T6 --scores can reuse internal/scorecard.ReadAll + Aggregate (or ApplyFilters) rather than inventing a new aggregation path. The scorecard store lives at ~/.config/atcr/scorecard/YYYY-MM.jsonl.

RESOLVED 2026-06-24 (was: open design point) — corroboration scores reach SelectEligibleSkeptics via a caller-supplied scores map[string]float64 4th parameter (reviewer name -> corroboration rate), nil-safe (nil = alphabetical-only tie-break). Chosen over functional option (avoids Option machinery for one optional input) and new overload (avoids a first-class scoreless path). Only one production caller (internal/verify/pipeline.go:162). The same map shape is built from scorecard.Aggregate() (LeaderboardRow.CorroborationRate keyed by reviewer) and shared with T6's --scores consumer, so verify gains no scorecard import.

RESOLVED 2026-06-24 — AgentConfig.Language canonical form is WITHOUT leading dot, lowercased (e.g. ["go","ts"]). YAML accepts both "go" and ".go"; applyDefaults canonicalizes each entry (trim space, strip one leading dot, lowercase), mirroring the existing MinSeverity/Role load-time canonicalization. validateAgent rejects empty entries + control chars (mirror the Scope guard) but enforces no language allow-list. Matching uses normalizeExt(ext) = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimPrefix(ext, ".")) on filepath.Ext(finding.File) so both sides compare in the same canonical form.
